gaps.title: Unity editor, asset store, learning, profiling, collaboration, and LiveOps ecosystem maturity
gaps.competitorAdvantage: Unity has 20+ years of editor maturity, 70K Asset Store assets, Unity Learn certifications, integrated Profiler + Frame Debugger, Plastic SCM collaboration, and a full LiveOps SaaS stack (Analytics, Remote Config, Cloud Build, Multiplay). This ecosystem lock-in is the primary reason teams choose Unity even when the runtime is not optimal for their use case.
gaps.holoscriptState: Editor: Studio is beta; lacks Scene/Game/Inspector/Hierarchy/Project window parity. Asset Store: No marketplace at scale. Learning: Docs + video-tutorials exist, but no structured certification or university partnerships. Profiler: Synthetic benchmarks only; no real-time in-editor profiler. Collaboration: CRDT multi-user editing exists but no Plastic/Git LFS large-asset workflow or cloud project sharing. LiveOps: No Analytics, Remote Config, Cloud Build, Multiplay, or User Reporting equivalents.
gaps.neededResponse: 1. Editor: Prioritize Studio polish — terrain editor, animation timeline, visual scripting graph. 2. Asset Store: Seed with 500 high-quality simulation assets for verticals Unity ignores. 3. Learning: Launch HoloScript Academy with certifications and university partnerships. 4. Profiler: Build real-time profiler into Studio. 5. Collaboration: Extend CRDT with branch/merge for scenes and Git LFS. 6. LiveOps: Document integration paths to PlayFab/GameAnalytics/AWS GameLift rather than building from scratch.

nmos.vitality: healthy

verticals.holoscript_gap: three-tier DispatchPolicy not yet shipped; ZKML inference receipts not yet wired

gaps.title: NN-primary runtime with CPU-verifier backup — inverted stack ownership
gaps.competitorAdvantage: Game engines are CPU-primary stacks that bolt on AI as an add-on (Unity ML-Agents, Unreal Engine ML). NN frameworks are compute substrates with no semantic source language — the program lives in Python scripts and notebook cells, not in a portable, versioned, attestable composition format.
gaps.holoscriptState: HoloScript inverts the stack: NN (spiking neural network on WebGPU/neuromorphic, plus LLM speculation) is the primary runtime; CPU AST/typecheck/CURE verification is the backup/referee. Tier 1: SNN hot path for spatial/perceptual/animation traits (sub-ms latency, single-digit mW on neuromorphic hardware). Tier 2: LLM speculative warm path for semantic/agentic/compositional traits (CPU verifies via SimulationContract). Tier 3: CPU cold path for safety-critical, low-confidence-NN, audit, or replay scenarios.
gaps.neededResponse: Ship a three-tier DispatchPolicy in core that routes per-trait by (trait class × confidence × safety-criticality). Benchmark acceptance rate (α) the way speculative decoding literature does. Emit SimulationContract evidence packs on every dispatch. Add ZKML inference receipts on audit/replay paths. Publish energy-budget metadata so Tier 1 defaults to SNN, not LLM.
evidence: packages/snn-webgpu/ ships LIF simulator, prophetic-gi orchestrator, tropical activation, and paper-grade LIFDeterminismProbe.
packages/core/src/compiler/AgentInferenceExportTarget.ts + LLMProviderCapabilitiesCompiler.ts + ContextCompiler.ts + EffectInference.ts give the compiler-side pieces.
NIR export target is registered for neuromorphic compile targets.
2026 speculative-decoding literature (ICML Intel/Weizmann, Dovetail EMNLP) gives prior art and 2–3× speedup metrics for the verifier pattern.
SpiNNaker digital event-driven SNN reproducibility ≤1% conversion loss; Yao et al. 2026 formal verification toolchain for probabilistic SNNs.
ZKML survey 2026: DeepSeek-V3-scale proofs with constant proof size and constant verification time.

Document ingest workflow: Luma AI capture → .splat → HoloScript import → physics annotation → multi-target compile. 3. Demo: real room captured → HoloScript → VRChat world with cloth/fluid physics. This is the 'scan the real world, simulate it, deploy anywhere' story.

nmos.vitality: healthy — ROS 2 is the standard robot OS; Gazebo Harmonic active; nearly every robotics team uses URDF/SDF

verticals.holoscript_superiority: semantic layer above URDF/SDF (HoloScript compiles TO both); SimulationContract receipts for sim-to-real verification; multi-domain physics solvers beyond Gazebo's ODE/Bullet defaults
verticals.holoscript_gap: no ROS 2 workspace integration guide; no ros2_control→HoloScript round-trip; no Gazebo Harmonic world generation tutorial

gaps.title: HoloScript is the semantic layer above ROS 2 / URDF / SDF — no integration guide exists
gaps.competitorAdvantage: URDF and SDF are the de facto standards for robot description. Every ROS 2 team uses them. Gazebo Harmonic is the standard simulator. The ecosystem lock-in is the URDF/SDF file format, not the tools.
gaps.holoscriptState: URDFCompiler.ts + SDFCompiler.ts are mature compile targets. URDF/SDF output is used as the bridge to ROS 2 and Gazebo. HoloScript is the semantic source; URDF/SDF are the standard outputs. No integration guide. No ROS 2 package example.
gaps.neededResponse: 1. Write 'HoloScript for ROS 2 developers' guide: .holo robot → compile_to_urdf → ROS 2 workspace. 2. Publish as ROS 2 community resource (ROS Discourse + GitHub robotics-worldwide). 3. Isaac Sim integration (docs/targets/usd-omniverse.md) covers the USD path; add URDF path explicitly. 4. Position: 'Write your robot in HoloScript once, deploy to Gazebo, Isaac Sim, and real hardware from the same source.'
evidence: docs/targets/ISAAC_SIM_OPTIMIZATION.md — URDF path to Isaac Sim

verticals.vertical: Hardware, edge AI, and embedded development

nmos.vitality: healthy — Jetson Orin is embedded AI standard; Isaac platform growing

verticals.holoscript_superiority: cross-hardware semantic evidence layer; one composition compiles to Jetson + Loihi + Qualcomm + cloud; provenance portable across vendor runtimes
verticals.holoscript_gap: no hardware receipt types for Jetson-specific runtime metrics; no JetPack SDK integration guide

gaps.title: HoloScript can become the cross-hardware semantic evidence layer competitors do not provide
gaps.competitorAdvantage: Hardware vendors optimize their own modules, devices, runtimes, and deployment funnels.
gaps.holoscriptState: HoloScript can describe intent once across robotics, IoT, twins, spatial apps, agents, and service targets, then attach provenance and validation evidence across vendor-specific hardware lanes.
gaps.neededResponse: Protect the differentiator by making hardware receipts portable: target, device, runtime, compiler version, constraints, measured results, replay inputs, provenance, and owner must be recorded consistently.
evidence: Jetson and Qualcomm sources show strong vendor-specific edge AI lanes.
HoloScript export target and definition sources show broader cross-domain semantic ambition.

nmos.vitality: healthy — neuromorphic hardware investment growing; NIR standard emerging

verticals.holoscript_superiority: NIRCompiler provides unified neuromorphic IR targeting all three platforms; WGSL fallback path for non-neuromorphic deployment; SNN WebGPU package for browser-native spiking networks
verticals.holoscript_gap: NIR spec evolving; receipt types per neuromorphic runtime needed

gaps.title: NIR compiler ships for neuromorphic targets
gaps.competitorAdvantage: Each neuromorphic platform has its own SDK, toolchain, and verification workflow.
gaps.holoscriptState: NIRCompiler + NIRToWGSLCompiler provide a unified neuromorphic intermediate representation that targets Intel Loihi 2, SpiNNaker 2, and SynSense.
gaps.neededResponse: Maintain parity as NIR spec evolves. Add receipt types for each neuromorphic runtime.
evidence: NIRCompiler.ts exists and compiles .holo to NIR.
QualcommNIRModelExportReceipt provides on-device metrics for Snapdragon NIR deployments.

nmos.vitality: healthy niche — do-calculus and causal inference growing in ML research; Microsoft DoWhy + Uber CausalML are active

verticals.holoscript_superiority: SCMCompiler exports HoloScript compositions as do-calculus intervention DAGs — HoloScript simulations become causal training data; SimulationContract receipts give causal claims verifiable provenance
verticals.holoscript_gap: SCM export workflow undocumented; no ML pipeline integration guide; causal ML community unaware HoloScript generates SCM-compatible output

gaps.title: SCMCompiler positions HoloScript as a causal training data generator — undocumented niche
gaps.competitorAdvantage: DoWhy (Microsoft) and CausalML (Uber) are the dominant causal inference frameworks. For ML teams needing intervention data, these are standard tools. Neither generates physically grounded simulation data.
gaps.holoscriptState: SCMCompiler.ts exports HoloScript compositions to JSON causal DAG formats supporting do-calculus interventions. HoloScript physics simulations can generate causally-labeled training datasets with SimulationContract provenance. No documentation, no ML community presence.
gaps.neededResponse: 1. Document SCM export workflow for ML researchers. 2. Demo: HoloScript rigid body simulation → SCM DAG → DoWhy causal inference. 3. Position: 'Physically grounded causal training data with verifiable provenance.' Paper candidate — pairs with Papers 17-20 (ML experiments).

nmos.note: NFTMarketplaceCompiler emits ERC-1155 Solidity — HoloScript Protocol is the sovereign alternative for in-world HoloScript asset economies. BUILD-INTERNAL for HoloScript's own creator economy.

verticals.holoscript_superiority: HoloScript Protocol is the sovereign on-chain economy for HoloScript worlds — creator share 70%+ vs OpenSea's model; SimulationContract provenance for verified digital assets
verticals.holoscript_gap: holo_protocol tools not yet widely adopted; no external NFT marketplace integration guide

gaps.title: NFT marketplace — WATCH + BUILD-INTERNAL (HoloScript Protocol is the sovereign answer)
gaps.competitorAdvantage: ERC-1155 + OpenSea were the dominant NFT infrastructure. Now declining but the on-chain digital asset pattern survives in specific gaming/virtual world contexts.
gaps.holoscriptState: NFTMarketplaceCompiler.ts emits ERC-1155 Solidity contracts. HoloScript Protocol (holo_protocol_* tools) is the sovereign economy layer for HoloScript worlds. For in-world assets, Protocol > OpenSea.
gaps.neededResponse: 1. Document when to use HoloScript Protocol vs NFTMarketplaceCompiler. 2. Pattern harvest: what survived the NFT crash (gaming assets, concert tickets, verifiable digital provenance). 3. HoloScript Protocol as the on-chain economy for HoloLand — this is BUILD-INTERNAL, not bridge.

nmos.vitality: vendor dead (Google discontinued Cardboard); sentiment alive (15M+ Cardboard units = real demand; demand unmet by $499 Quest)
nmos.note: PhoneSleeveVRCompiler exists — HoloScript is the substrate for the sovereign revival. D.037 + D.041 (prone-bed) + I.012 (Revival Scan Initiative).

verticals.holoscript_superiority: PhoneSleeveVRCompiler generates WebXR stereoscopic HTML from .holo compositions; AI-native content generation fills the quality gap that killed Cardboard; NN-primary perception handles phone sensor limitations
verticals.holoscript_gap: no consumer-facing PhoneSleeve product yet; revival is architecture-complete at compiler layer but not marketed

gaps.title: PhoneSleeveVR sovereign revival — compiler exists, no product or marketing yet
gaps.competitorAdvantage: Google Cardboard reached 15M+ units proving the $0-device-cost market segment. $499+ Quest cannot reach this segment. The category died for vendor-strategic reasons, not fundamental ones.
gaps.holoscriptState: PhoneSleeveVRCompiler.ts generates self-contained WebXR HTML for stereoscopic VR on phone-sleeve headsets. D.037 classified this as sovereign-revival-target. D.041 (prone-bed ease-of-play) maps to the same hardware. Missing innovation arrived: AI content generation + WebXR + NN-primary perception.
gaps.neededResponse: 1. Demo: HoloScript scene → compile_to_phonesleevevr → QR code → any phone in a $15 sleeve. 2. Target: gaming cafes, education, emerging markets ($499 Quest is out of reach). 3. Pairs with D.041 prone-bed: face-down VR on a phone sleeve is the most accessible form factor.
